# Harvester Guide

The harvester is the core gathering tool. This guide explains yield modifiers,
resource quality tiers, and the upgrade path so a gathering session feels
rewarding from the first node to the last.

## Yield Modifiers

Yield is the amount of raw resource a single harvest returns. The yield modifier
stacks additively from the tool tier and multiplicatively from the node richness.
A tier-three harvester on a rich ore node returns substantially more ore per
swing than a tier-one harvester on a poor node. Yield never depends on player
level directly; it is entirely a function of tool and node.

## Resource Quality Tiers

Resource quality runs across five tiers: crude, common, fine, pristine, and
flawless. Higher quality tiers gate the recipes a crafter can attempt. Quality
is rolled per harvest against the node's quality band, so a rich node still
occasionally yields a crude result. Crafters pay a premium for pristine and
flawless stock because the failure rate on high-end recipes drops sharply.

## Upgrade Path

Upgrading the harvester consumes credits and refined ingots. Each tier widens
the node richness the tool can profitably work and nudges the quality band
upward. The upgrade path is deliberately long so that the gatherer economy and
the crafter economy stay interdependent across the whole progression.
